---
title: "predictHousePrice_visualizations"
author: "Justin Yeo"
date: "2026-05-02"
output: pdf_document
---

Visualizations for comparing Yearly average mortgage rate against Yearly average HPI:

```{R}
library(tidyverse)

names = c("Year","Average","Lowest","Highest", "numberOfWeeks")

median = read_csv("cleaned_mortgage_rates_annual_avg.csv", col_names = names)
median = median %>% slice(5:nrow(median))

house = read_csv("CASTHPI_yearly_cleaned.csv")
house = house %>% select(-Year)

combined = cbind(median, house)

combined$Year <- as.numeric(as.character(combined$Year))
combined$Yearly_Average_HPI <- as.numeric(as.character(combined$Yearly_Average_HPI))
combined$Average <- as.numeric(as.character(combined$Average))

min_max <- function(x) (x - min(x, na.rm=TRUE)) / (max(x, na.rm=TRUE) - min(x, na.rm=TRUE))

combined$Average <- min_max(combined$Average)
combined$Yearly_Average_HPI<- min_max(combined$Yearly_Average_HPI)

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = combined, aes(x = Year, y = Yearly_Average_HPI, color = "Yearly HPI")) +
  geom_point(data = combined, aes(x = Year, y = Average, color = "Average")) +
  scale_color_manual("", values = c("Yearly HPI" = "blue", "Average" = "green")) +
  labs(title = "Year vs avg_mortgage_rate_30yr_pct (Average), Year vs Yearly HPI", subtitle = "normalized using max-min formula")

ggplot()+ 
  geom_point(data = combined, aes(x = Year, y = Average)) + 
  labs(title = "Year vs avg_mortgage_rate_30yr_pct (Average)")
```



Why a Linear Model will not work for this predictor: Adjusted R^2 is low, QQ plot and residuals plot indicates high outliers. 

```{R}
cor(combined$Average, combined$Yearly_Average_HPI)

model = lm(combined$Yearly_Average_HPI ~ combined$Average) 

summary(model)

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
plot(model)
par(mfrow = c(1,1))
```
